summary: IMPL_REVIEW confirmation round (impl-review-1) ran after operator applied the 7-commit fix batch addressing the 4 original critical blockers (F1/F2/F3/F5) plus the medium unconfirmed findings. The 4 confirmed cross-vendor critical blockers from impl-review-0 NONE reappear in any of the 3 rounds — fixes landed cleanly. Round 3 quorum_lost (claude_code + codex CLI errors) prevented consensus synthesis, so the result file shows blocking_count=0 procedurally. Round 2 (full 3/3 quorum, the reliable signal) surfaced 2 NEW cross-vendor-confirmed high-severity issues that were NOT present in impl-review-0 — both relating to SSE infrastructure that this round did not touch. Two trivial id=15/id=16 follow-ups applied in a final commit.

rounds.notes: claude_code timed out at 300s. codex 6 findings, gemini 4. All 10 surviving blockers were UNCONFIRMED (single-vendor).
FULL 3/3 quorum — the reliable signal of this confirmation run. 2 NEW confirmed cross-vendor blockers surface; none of the 4 original F1-F5 blockers reappear.

quorum_lost (1/3 < 2). Consensus synthesizer did not run; the convergence_loop's reported blocking_count=0 is procedural, not substantive. Treat R2's 18 findings + 2 confirmed blockers as the authoritative signal.

new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.description: SSE generator leaks EventBusService callbacks on every disconnect. event_bus.on_event('coordinator_task', _on_task_event) and event_bus.on_event('coordinator_audit', _on_audit_event) register callbacks but never unregister them when the client disconnects. Closed connections leave callback closures and queue references in the bus.
new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.recommended_fix: Wrap the generator body in try/finally that calls event_bus.off_event(...) for both registered callbacks; also clear the queue and break out of the while loop on disconnect.
new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.status_in_this_round: DEFERRED — out of scope per user's 'critical confirmed + medium unconfirmed' instruction (this is high-severity newly-surfaced).

new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.description: GET /events/work accepts the SSE JWT in the URL (?token=) without the contracted token-in-URL mitigations: (a) no access-log redaction of the token query parameter; (b) the EventSourceResponse is returned without overriding the default uvicorn access logger to mask the token. The JWT lands in standard access logs.
new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.recommended_fix: Add a Starlette middleware that rewrites query strings in access logs for /events/work to replace token=<value> with token=<redacted>. Alternative: switch from query-string token to a cookie or POST-mint-then-Authorization-header pattern.
new_confirmed_blockers_from_impl_review_.status_in_this_round: DEFERRED — out of scope per user's instruction. Pre-existing finding from impl-review-0 claude_code#10.

fixes_applied_in_impl_review_1_round.description: App.tsx mounted SyncPointBanner in commit e24e0c1 but didn't thread onAuditEmit, so destructive-write kicks emitted to nowhere — violating proposal §3 audit contract.
fixes_applied_in_impl_review_1_round.fix_description: App.tsx defines emitAudit useCallback POSTing to /kanban-viz/audit; threaded to SyncPointBanner.

fixes_applied_in_impl_review_1_round.description: Done column 24h filter used updated_at ?? created_at instead of completed_at per proposal §3.
fixes_applied_in_impl_review_1_round.fix_description: Added completed_at to Issue TS type (backend already emits via Issue.to_dict); Board.tsx uses completed_at-first fallback chain; fixtures updated.

other_unconfirmed_findings_logged_for_fo: R2-id=1 (high spec_gap): vendor swimlanes don't surface audit_log live activity per vendor — AgentActivity needs richer projection from audit-event stream
R2-id=2 (high contract_mismatch): frontend Issue TS type drift vs coordinator runtime shape — change_id/updated_at/task_key declared required but may not be populated
R2-id=3 (high spec_gap): saved-view path doesn't emit UI audit event file per task 6.3
R2-id=5/6 (medium compatibility/contract_mismatch): skills/tests/agent-coordinator/test_kanban_viz_endpoints.py dead-code dupe still exists (OC-1); sync_points.py reimplements check_no_active_agents
R2-id=7 (medium correctness): POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick reports kicked=true for REMOVED=skipped no-ops
R2-id=8 (medium architecture): sync_points.py materializes one status_row 3x — per-skill state conflated
R2-id=9 (medium correctness): Tauri saveView writes git_sha='0000000' placeholder per contracts/README spec violation
R2-id=10 (medium observability): snapshot/worktrees/sync-point endpoints silently degrade in Docker
R2-id=22 (medium architecture): saveView/drag-to-Ready/force-release-lock UI surfaces still not wired (already documented in tasks.md per commit 62fcb15)
codex#4 / claude_code#8 (high, from impl-review-0): NOTIFY trigger payloads lack change_id / from_status / to_status — SSE filter drops all real events

claude_code.notes: R1 timeout 300s; R3 instant error. Only R2 produced findings.

codex.notes: R3 stdin issue (model: gpt-5.5 stuck reading stdin). R1+R2 successful.

gemini.notes: Most reliable vendor this round. Only vendor to participate in all 3 rounds.

operator_recommendation: Advance to VALIDATE. The 4 original confirmed criticals are resolved (none reappear). The 2 new confirmed highs (R2-id=4 SSE callback leak, R2-id=13/20 token-in-URL redaction) are pre-existing infrastructure concerns that warrant a separate follow-up — they don't gate the MVP visualization features F1-F5, and the user's instruction was 'critical confirmed + medium unconfirmed' which scoped the impl-review-0 deltas. VALIDATE will surface anything else (spec, evidence, deploy, smoke, security, e2e). If VALIDATE fails on the token-in-URL issue, we can address it in a follow-up commit before SUBMIT_PR.
